title: Minimum Salary Changes Announced | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
## Minimum Salary Changes Announced

### Countries / Territories
The following countries have announced changes in salary requirements:
Updated December 24, 2025
Effective January 2026, the national minimum wage increased to ALL 50,000 gross per month – up from ALL 40,000.
Other relevant considerations:
- Market salary . Foreign workers must receive the market salary rate for the proposed minimum wage level.
- Compliance. The new requirements will apply to current permit holders, as well as all new, pending or renewal applications.
- Benefits and allowances. Benefits and allowances may only be included in the minimum salary calculation if they are specified in the employment contract, guaranteed and fixed, and are not paid in kind.
- Currency. Employers must ensure that the salary threshold in ALL is met, regardless of payroll location and/or exchange rate fluctuations.
- Cost of living. The foreign national’s salary must be sufficient to cover the cost of living of the principal and any accompanying dependents. Applicants must submit bank statements with their residence permit application to demonstrate access to sufficient funds.
Updated January 5, 2026
Work permit minimum salary levels. Effective January 1, 2026:
- Red-White-Red Card (key employee): EUR 3,465 gross per month, up from 3,225 gross per month, unless the minimum salary level under any applicable collective bargaining agreement requires a higher amount.
- EU Blue Card: 55,678 gross per year, unless the minimum salary level under any applicable collective bargaining agreement requires a higher amount.
- Super key employee: EUR 8,316 gross per month, up from EUR 7,740
- LSD-BG (Austrian Wage and Social Dumping Combating Act) exemption: EUR 8,316 gross per month, up from EUR 7,740
Collective bargaining agreements. In the following situations, minimum salaries are not determined by the above figures, but rather by the applicable collective bargaining agreement for the role in question:
- Short-Term Work Permit;
- Short-Term Secondment Permit; EU Intracompany Transferee (ICT) Permit;
- Red-White-Red Card for professionals in shortage occupations; and
- Red-White-Red Card holders/applicants who are graduates from Austrian universities.
Benefits and allowances​ typically cannot be included in the minimum salary calculation under collective bargaining agreements.
Other relevant considerations:
- Existing employees. Employers of foreign nationals currently under a Red-White-Red Card do not need to increase a foreign national’s salary to comply with any new salary level for the card. However, they do have to account for any salary increases under any relevant collective bargaining arrangement.
- 14 payments per year. Salaries must be paid in 14 installments per year.
- Financial capacity : Single individuals . Must have at least net EUR 1,308.39 per month, after all deductions. Dependents . If dependents are accompanying the third-country national, the income must be sufficient to provide for the family. Rent payments are to be deducted when considering whether a candidate meets such thresholds. For married partners it is EUR 2.064,12. For each child, an additional net amount of EUR 201.88 is required.
- Single individuals . Must have at least net EUR 1,308.39 per month, after all deductions. Dependents . If dependents are accompanying the third-country national, the income must be sufficient to provide for the family. Rent payments are to be deducted when considering whether a candidate meets such thresholds. For married partners it is EUR 2.064,12. For each child, an additional net amount of EUR 201.88 is required.
- Single individuals . Must have at least net EUR 1,308.39 per month, after all deductions.
- Dependents . If dependents are accompanying the third-country national, the income must be sufficient to provide for the family. Rent payments are to be deducted when considering whether a candidate meets such thresholds. For married partners it is EUR 2.064,12. For each child, an additional net amount of EUR 201.88 is required.
- Posted worker notification requirements . The following employees, posted by an employer based in the EU/EEA/Switzerland, are exempt from posted worker notification requirements (and certain document keeping requirements): Employees with a gross monthly salary of at least EUR 8,316 (prior and during the posting), up from EUR 7,740.
- Employees with a gross monthly salary of at least EUR 8,316 (prior and during the posting), up from EUR 7,740.
- Employees with a gross monthly salary of at least EUR 8,316 (prior and during the posting), up from EUR 7,740.
- Currency . Employers must guarantee the salary in EUR regardless of payroll location and/or exchange rate fluctuations.
Updated March 9, 2026
As of July 1, 2025, the national minimum wage increased to AUD 948 per week (or AUD 24.95 per hour).
Effective July 1, 2024, the Temporary Skilled Migration Income Threshold (TSMIT) for employer-sponsored visa programs increased from AUD 70,000 to AUD 73,150.
On December 7, 2024, the Skills in Demand (SID) visa was introduced, along with two new salary thresholds:
- Core Skills Income Threshold: AUD 73,150, applicable to the Core Skills stream of the SID visa and the Temporary Residence Transition and Direct Entry streams of the Employer Nomination Scheme visa.
- Specialist Skills Income Threshold: AUD 135,000, applicable to the Specialist Skills stream of the SID visa.
Starting July 1, 2025, the following thresholds will apply:
- Core Skills Income Threshold: AUD 76,515 (up from AUD 73,150)
- Specialist Skills Income Threshold: AUD 141,210 (up from AUD 135,000)
- Temporary Skilled Migration Income Threshold : AUD 76,515 (up from AUD 73,150). This applies to the Skilled Employer Sponsored Regional (subclass 494) visa and may also affect Labour Agreements under the subclass 482 visa.
- High Income Threshold: AUD 183,100 (up from AUD 175,000).
- Mandatory superannuation contributions: increased from 11.5% to 12%. Employers must ensure that SID/Temporary Skills Shortage visa holders continue to receive at least the approved guaranteed earnings in accordance with prevailing market rates despite this increase.
Update – March 9, 2026
Based on the prescribed indexation formula, our preliminary calculations indicate the following thresholds will apply from July 1, 2026:
- Core Skills Income Threshold: AUD 79,423 (up from AUD 76,515)
- Specialist Skills Income Threshold: AUD 146,576 (up from AUD 141,210)
- The Temporary Skills Migration Income Threshold (TSMIT) , applicable to regional sponsored visa programs (subclass 494 and subclass 187), is not subject to the same indexation formula, and changes to the TSMIT are expected later this year, through a legislative instrument.
The figures mentioned above are subject to formal confirmation by the Department of Home Affairs, which is expected to publish the updated thresholds on its website in due course.
Updated January 7, 2025
Effective January 1, 2025, the national minimum wage increased to AZN 400 per month, up from AZN 345.
- Affected categories. The increase affects all foreign nationals who must be paid at least the minimum wage; however, there is no minimum salary level for the work permit category as long as it is above the minimum wage. Compliance. In case of threshold changes, salaries of current work permit holders and new applications must all meet the new threshold. Benefits and allowances. Benefits and allowances cannot be included in the minimum salary calculation.
- Affected categories. The increase affects all foreign nationals who must be paid at least the minimum wage; however, there is no minimum salary level for the work permit category as long as it is above the minimum wage.
- Compliance. In case of threshold changes, salaries of current work permit holders and new applications must all meet the new threshold.
- Benefits and allowances. Benefits and allowances cannot be included in the minimum salary calculation.
Bangladesh’s minimum salary level requirements vary and are based on nationality and job designation. They range from USD 1200 or BDT equivalent (up from USD 650) for skilled workers to USD 3,000 or BDT equivalent (up from USD 2,000) for investors and executives. The E Visa and Work Permit category is subject to these minimum salary requirements.
Updated December 3, 2025
The minimum salary levels for Special Work Permit and Temporary Residence Permit applications are:
- Highly qualified employees : Effective January 1, 2026, the minimum salary level for ‘highly qualified employees’ is BYN 4,290 per month (five times the minimum wage of BYN 858 per month) – up from BYN 3,630.
- Highly qualified employees : Effective January 1, 2026, the minimum salary level for ‘highly qualified employees’ is BYN 4,290 per month (five times the minimum wage of BYN 858 per month) – up from BYN 3,630.
- Other applicants : Effective January 1, 2026, the migration authorities recommend that the minimum salary level for other foreign nationals be at least BYN 2,250 per month – up from BYN 2,100.
- Other applicants : Effective January 1, 2026, the migration authorities recommend that the minimum salary level for other foreign nationals be at least BYN 2,250 per month – up from BYN 2,100.
- Dependents : Sponsors of dependents (including dependent spouses and children) must also earn a minimum subsistence amount for each dependent. These amounts – which vary depending on the type of dependent, among other factors – are revised quarterly. Affected individuals should contact their immigration professional for further details.
- Dependents : Sponsors of dependents (including dependent spouses and children) must also earn a minimum subsistence amount for each dependent. These amounts – which vary depending on the type of dependent, among other factors – are revised quarterly. Affected individuals should contact their immigration professional for further details.
Other relevant considerations:
- Compliance. The new amounts apply to pending and new applications.
- Local payroll and currency. Foreign workers must be on Belarussian payroll and employers pay the salary in BYN. The amount of the salary may be set in a foreign currency at the rate of the service bank on the day of payment.
- Social security. The employer contributes an additional 34% of the monthly salary as mandatory social security insurance payments. 1% of the monthly salary is withheld from the foreign national as a mandatory social security insurance payment. The employer also pays a mandatory accident insurance contribution of approximately 6% of the salary.
- Income tax. 13% of the salary is withheld from the foreign national as personal income tax (in some cases, the payer is eligible for tax deductions).
- General provisions. The above is based on general provisions within the legislation. In some cases, different features may be established by specific legislation.
Updated January 7, 2026
Flanders: Effective January 1, 2025, minimum salary thresholds are as follows (all entries are gross per year):
- Highly Skilled Permit: Executives: EUR 78,259, up from EUR 74,611. Highly skilled: EUR 48,912, up from EUR 46,632. Locally hired young graduates (under 30 years of age) or nurses: EUR 39,129.60, up from EUR 37,305.60.
- Executives: EUR 78,259, up from EUR 74,611.
- Highly skilled: EUR 48,912, up from EUR 46,632.
- Locally hired young graduates (under 30 years of age) or nurses: EUR 39,129.60, up from EUR 37,305.60.
- EU Blue Card: EUR 63,586, up from EUR 60,621.
- EU Intra-Company Transfer Permit Managers: EUR 78,259, up from EUR 74,611. Specialists and trainees: EUR 48,912, up from EUR 46,632.
- Managers: EUR 78,259, up from EUR 74,611. Specialists and trainees: EUR 48,912, up from EUR 46,632.
- Managers: EUR 78,259, up from EUR 74,611.
- Specialists and trainees: EUR 48,912, up from EUR 46,632.
The Flanders region has not yet announced new minimum salaries for 2026. We are continuing to monitor the situation.
Wallonia: Effective January 1, 2026, minimum salary thresholds will be as follows (all entries are gross per year):
- Highly Skilled Permit: Executives: EUR 88,790, up from EUR 86,110. Highly skilled: EUR 53,220, up from EUR 51,613. Employees under 30 years of age: EUR 42,576, up from EUR 41,290.
- Executives: EUR 88,790, up from EUR 86,110.
- Highly skilled: EUR 53,220, up from EUR 51,613.
- Employees under 30 years of age: EUR 42,576, up from EUR 41,290.
- EU Blue Card: General: EUR 68,815, up from EUR 66,738. Recent graduates (applied less than three years after higher education graduation): EUR 55,052, up from EUR 53,390.
- General: EUR 68,815, up from EUR 66,738.
- Recent graduates (applied less than three years after higher education graduation): EUR 55,052, up from EUR 53,390.
- EU Intra-Company Transfer Permit: Managers: EUR 68,815, up from EUR 66,738. Specialists: EUR 55,053, up from EUR 53,390. Trainees: EUR 34,408, up from EUR 33,370.
- Managers: EUR 68,815, up from EUR 66,738.
- Specialists: EUR 55,053, up from EUR 53,390.
- Trainees: EUR 34,408, up from EUR 33,370.
Since September 1, 2024, highly qualified permit types (for example, Highly Skilled Permits, EU Blue Cards, and EU Intra-Company Transfer Permits) have been pro-rated according to the time actually worked by the employee. This allows such permit holders to work part time without putting at risk the validity of their work permit. Note that the salary should still satisfy relevant labor law requirements (including the guaranteed average monthly income).
Brussels : The 2026 minimum salary thresholds for Brussels are the same as they were for 2025, being:
- Highly Skilled Permit: Executives: EUR 6,647,20 per month, up from EUR 6,445.60 per month. Highly skilled: EUR 3,703,44 per month up from EUR 3,591.12 per month.
- Executives: EUR 6,647,20 per month, up from EUR 6,445.60 per month. Highly skilled: EUR 3,703,44 per month up from EUR 3,591.12 per month.
- Executives: EUR 6,647,20 per month, up from EUR 6,445.60 per month.
- Highly skilled: EUR 3,703,44 per month up from EUR 3,591.12 per month.
- EU Blue Card: EUR 4,748, up from EUR 4,604 per month.
- EU Intra-Company Transfer Permit Managers: EUR 5,460.20 per month, up from EUR 5,294.60 per month. Specialists: EUR 4,510,60 per month, up from EUR 4,373.80 per month. Trainees: EUR 2.611,40 per month, up from EUR 2,532.20 per month.
- Managers: EUR 5,460.20 per month, up from EUR 5,294.60 per month.
- Specialists: EUR 4,510,60 per month, up from EUR 4,373.80 per month.
- Trainees: EUR 2.611,40 per month, up from EUR 2,532.20 per month.
Other relevant considerations include:
- Currency. Employers must guarantee the salary in EUR, regardless of payroll location or exchange rate fluctuations.
- Compliance. From the date of the effective change, the minimum salary levels for foreign workers holding any of the work authorizations listed above must increase to comply with the new rule.
- New, renewal and pending applications . From the date of the effective change, all foreign national work authorization applications (including renewals) must increase foreign nationals’ minimum salaries to comply with the new rule. This includes applications pending from before this date. Employers must also notify authorities about salary increases to such pending applications. Applications that do not meet the minimum salary will be rejected.
- Benefits and allowances. Benefits and cost allowances generally cannot be included in the minimum salary calculation. In exceptional cases, allowances can be included provided they are: (1) remuneration in return for work performed within the framework of the employment agreement; (2) guaranteed and fixed; (3) taxable; and (4) indicated in the employee’s pay slip.
Updated January 29, 2025
Botswana’s minimum wage thresholds remain unchanged, but are likely to increase in March/April. Key wages include:
- BWP 9.06 per hour for: Building, construction, exploration, and quarry industries; Wholesale distributive trades Manufacturing, service, and repair trades Hotel, catering and entertainment trades Garage, motor trade and road transport Guards who are not employed by security companies Retail distributive trade Guards employed by security companies
- Building, construction, exploration, and quarry industries;
- Wholesale distributive trades
- Manufacturing, service, and repair trades
- Hotel, catering and entertainment trades
- Garage, motor trade and road transport
- Guards who are not employed by security companies
- Retail distributive trade
- Guards employed by security companies
- BWP 1,500 per month for: Domestic services sector Agricultural sector
- Domestic services sector
UPDATED December 2, 2025:
Effective January 1, 2026, Bulgaria is adopting the euro currency. Minimum salary assessments will need to account for the change from BGN to EUR currencies.
Effective January 1, 2026, the national minimum wage will increase to EUR 620.20 per month – up from BGN 1,077 (which is around EUR 550).
- Affected categories. The increase affects the EU Intra-Company Transferee Permit, the Combined Permit and EU nationals. EU Blue Card applicants are subject to a different threshold.
- Compliance. In case of threshold changes, salaries of current permit holders, workers with pending applications and new applications must all meet the new threshold.​​
- Market salary rate. Applicants should receive no less that the salary offered to Bulgarian nationals hired in the company for the same category of work. The minimum wage serves as a final salary requirement.
- Benefits and allowances. Benefits and allowances cannot be included in the minimum salary calculation.
Updated July 2, 2025:
Effective June 27, 2025, Canada increased wage thresholds for the Temporary Foreign Worker Program (TFWP) by an average of just over 3%. These adjustments affect Labour Market Impact Assessment (LMIA) applications nationwide by redefining eligibility for the High- and Low-Wage streams. TFWP applications, including renewals, that don’t meet the updated High-Wage threshold will default to the Low-Wage stream, which carries stricter regulatory requirements .
Updated January 2, 2026
Effective January 1, 2026, the minimum wage in Colombia increased to COP 1,750,905 per month (approximately USD 460), a 23% percent increase from the 2025 minimum wage. The increase does not directly affect the hiring of foreign nationals from an immigration perspective; however, employers sponsoring work visas should ensure that even with the increase in the minimum wage, their financial statements for the past three, four or six months (depending on the visa type) still show a monthly average balance of at least 100 times the minimum wage. For labor purposes, a foreign national’s salary may not be less than the monthly minimum wage.
Updated December 19, 2025
Effective January 1, 2026, the national minimum wage will increase to EUR 1,050 gross per month, up from EUR 970.
- Affected categories . The increase affects EU nationals and general Stay and Work Permit applications. Stay and Work Permit applicants under the Key Personnel and EU Blue Card routes are subject to a separate salary threshold. Stay and Work Permit applicants under the EU Intra-Company Transferee Permit route should receive a salary comparable to locally hired staff in similar job roles (market salary rate). Posted workers’ salaries must be in line with the wage of a local worker according to Croatian law (collective bargaining agreement) if the law is more favorable than the employment agreement.
- Compliance . In case the threshold changes, employers must adjust the salaries of current foreign workers and those with pending or new applications, if required.
- Benefits and allowances . Benefits and allowances cannot be included in the minimum salary calculation.
Updated January 7, 2026
National minimum wage. Effective January 1, 2026, the national minimum wage (applicable only to Cypriot or EU nationals) is:
- Employees who have been with their employer for at least six months : EUR 1,088 gross per month (up from EUR 1,000).
- Employees who have been with their employer for less than six months: EUR 979 gross per month (up from EUR 900).
Local Hire permit category . As of January 1, 2024 (and still valid as of January 2026) the minimum salary level for the Local Hire permit category (applicable to non-EU foreign workers) is:
- Roles excluding executive directors : EUR 2,000-3,871 gross per month (salary level depends on the seniority of the role).
- Executive directors : EUR 3,872 gross per month.
EU Blue Card . As of July 2025 (and still valid as of January 2026), annual gross salary of EUR 43,632.
Other relevant conditions:
- Benefits and allowances. Benefits and allowances cannot be included in the minimum salary calculation.
- Intra-corporate transferees, and posted workers. There are no minimum salary requirements for either Cyprus’s EU ICT permit or its Posted Worker permit.
Updated March 25, 2026
Employee Card and Work Permit/Employee Card:
Effective January 1, 2026, the minimum salary thresholds for:
- the Employee Card and Work Permit/Employee Card will increase to CZK 22,400 per month, up from the current CZK 20,800 .
- the Qualified Employee Migration Program will increase to CZK 27,328 per month, up from the current CZK 25,376.
Sponsorship. The minimum amount required for dependent sponsorship for all permit types will remain CZK 3,130. Applicants sponsoring dependent family must hold 15 times the minimum amount for the first month and twice the minimum for every other month of stay.
Sponsorship and subsistence requirements . As of January 1, 2025, sponsorship and subsistence requirements are as follows (and will not change in 2026):
- Sponsoring a six-month stay , applicants must hold CZK 78,250 per adult dependent and CZK 39,125 per dependent under 18.
- Sponsoring a one-year stay , applicants must now hold CZK 115,810 per adult dependent or CZK 57,905 per dependent under 18.
Long-term business visa applicants must hold CZK 156,500 per month for each month of the visa validity period. The full required amount must be available in a bank account when filing the application.
EU Blue Card. Effective May 1, 2026, the gross monthly salary for EU Bleu Cards will increase to CZK 73,833.
Effective May 1, 2025, the gross monthly salary for EU Blue Cards increased to CZK 69,248.
- Formalities . The amount must be specified in the employment contract.
- Benefits and allowances . Benefits and bonuses are not included in the calculation.
- Minimum subsistence amount . Applicants seeking to sponsor dependents must additionally hold the abovementioned minimum subsistence amounts.
- Compliance . In case of threshold changes, the new amount applies to current employees, pending applications, and new or renewal applications.
Updated December 18, 2025
Pay limit applications. Effective January 1, 2026, the gross minimum salary level for the following categories has increased:
- Pay Limit Scheme applications (including those filed under the Fast-Track Scheme): DKK 552,000 per year, up from DKK 514,000.
- Supplementary Pay Limit Scheme applications : DKK 446,000 per year, up from DKK 415,000.
Other relevant considerations:
- Currency . All amounts must be reflected in DKK in the employment contract, and if the salary is stated in a foreign currency, an addendum stating the salary in DKK must be submitted.
- Initial applications . Employers of foreign nationals seeking to obtain a Work Permit after these minimum salary level changes must increase the foreign national’s salary to comply with the new rule. Immigration applications that do not meet the minimum salary will be refused.
- Pending applications . These minimum salary level changes do not affect pending applications submitted prior to the change.
- Renewal applications. The minimum salary level for renewal applications remains the same as the minimum salary for the year the original application was submitted.
- Benefits and allowances . Benefits and allowances cannot be included in the minimum salary calculation. Only basic salary, paid holiday allowance and payments to the labor market pension scheme can be included in the minimum salary calculation.
- DISCO code: Initial and renewal applications must have a salary and Danish International Standard Classification of Occupations (DISCO) code that corresponds to the applicable Danish Income Statistic.
- Payment in a Danish bank account. The foreign national’s full salary must be paid into a Danish bank account when working in Denmark for more than six months. For Fast-Track cases, the salary does not need to be paid out to a Danish bank account .
Updated April 15, 2025
Effective March 1, 2025, the minimum salary for all employees in Egypt’s private sector increased to EGP 7,000 per month, up from EGP 6,000.
Updated April 7, 2026
Minimum Wage : Effective April 1, 2026, the minimum wage is EUR 946 per month (up from EUR 886).
Residence Permit for Employment & Short-Term Employment Program category:
- Top specialists : Effective March 6, 2026, at least EUR 3,138 per month (up from EUR 2,972), or the average for other employees in the local company, whichever is higher. Other specialists (experts, advisors, consultants): Effective March 6, 2026, EUR 2,092 per month (up from EUR 1,981).
- Top specialists : Effective March 6, 2026, at least EUR 3,138 per month (up from EUR 2,972), or the average for other employees in the local company, whichever is higher. Other specialists (experts, advisors, consultants): Effective March 6, 2026, EUR 2,092 per month (up from EUR 1,981).
- Top specialists : Effective March 6, 2026, at least EUR 3,138 per month (up from EUR 2,972), or the average for other employees in the local company, whichever is higher.
- Other specialists (experts, advisors, consultants): Effective March 6, 2026, EUR 2,092 per month (up from EUR 1,981).
EU Blue Card : Effective March 6, 2025 (and still valid as of March 2026):
- Standard : EUR 3,096 per month (up from EUR 2,748); and ‘Sought-after’ occupations : EUR 2,972 per month (up from EUR 2,272).
- Standard : EUR 3,096 per month (up from EUR 2,748); and ‘Sought-after’ occupations : EUR 2,972 per month (up from EUR 2,272).
- Standard : EUR 3,096 per month (up from EUR 2,748); and
- ‘Sought-after’ occupations : EUR 2,972 per month (up from EUR 2,272).
Other relevant considerations:
- Additional fund requirements per spouse and family member (both under and over 18 years of age) also apply. Compliance . Current permit holders and pending applications continue to fall under former thresholds. Salaries need only be indexed when filing a new / renewal application. Benefits and allowances . Benefits cannot be included in the calculation of the threshold. Currency . Employers are required to guarantee the salary in EUR regardless of payroll location and/or exchange rate fluctuations.
- Additional fund requirements per spouse and family member (both under and over 18 years of age) also apply. Compliance . Current permit holders and pending applications continue to fall under former thresholds. Salaries need only be indexed when filing a new / renewal application. Benefits and allowances . Benefits cannot be included in the calculation of the threshold. Currency . Employers are required to guarantee the salary in EUR regardless of payroll location and/or exchange rate fluctuations.
- Additional fund requirements per spouse and family member (both under and over 18 years of age) also apply.
- Compliance . Current permit holders and pending applications continue to fall under former thresholds. Salaries need only be indexed when filing a new / renewal application.
- Benefits and allowances . Benefits cannot be included in the calculation of the threshold.
- Currency . Employers are required to guarantee the salary in EUR regardless of payroll location and/or exchange rate fluctuations.
Updated January 7, 2026
- Specialist Permits and Blue Cards: Effective January 1 , 2026, EUR 3,937 gross per month, up from EUR 3,827.
- Residence permit for an employed person: As of January 1, 2025, if there is no collective agreement or the work is part-time, the salary must now be at least EUR 1,600 gross per month – up from EUR 1,399.
As of January 19, 2023, authorities are using the Finnish Incomes Register to verify that applicants extending permits based on employment or studies are being paid a sufficient income, whereas previously applicants were required to provide copies of pay slips. This is expected to improve the reliability of information authorities use to adjudicate extension applications and will reduce the amount of information they typically require for the application.
Other relevant considerations:
- Benefits and allowances . Benefits and allowances cannot be included in the minimum salary calculation.
- Safe margin . Applicants paid close to the minimum salary threshold are likely to face clarification requests and/or refusals.
- Compliance . In case of threshold changes, the new threshold applies to current permit holders, pending and new applications. Uplifts must be completed at least three months before a renewal application is filed as renewal applications require payslips for the three months prior to the application.
Updated June 4, 2026
Minimum wage : Effective June 1, 2026, EUR 1,867.02 gross per month (up from EUR 1,823.03). This minimum wage applies to local hires and intra corporate transferees, among others, unless the relevant collective bargaining agreement has a higher income. Additional benefits and allowances may also apply.
- ‘Talent – Qualified Employee’: Effective August 31, 2025, EUR 39,582 per year (down from EUR 43,243.20 for new graduates and up from EUR 38,918.88 for employees on assignment). On June 13, 2025, this permit type was restructured . In the absence of a proper implementing act, the permit appeared at the time temporarily subject to a markedly lower minimum salary ( EUR 35,891) , as per a 2016 reference.
- Talent – EU Blue Card : Effective August 31, 2025, EUR 59,373 per year (up from EUR 53,836.50).
- Talent – Medical and Pharmacy : As of June 16, 2025, EUR 41,386.48 per year.
- Talent – Legal Representative (Mandataire Social) :  Effective June 1, 2026, EUR 67,212.72 per year (up from EUR 65,629.08).
- Job seeker/new business creator (Recherche d’emploi et création d’entreprise) : Effective June 1, 2026, EUR 33,606.36 per year (up from EUR 32,814.54).
Other relevant considerations:
- Benefits and allowances. Benefits and allowances may only be included if they are a fixed amount, taxable, subject to social security, and visible on the payslip.
- Compliance. The new thresholds apply to current permit holders, pending and new applications.
The minimum salary level increased to GEL 1,416.50 per month (up from GEL 1,301.50). This is five times the national minimum monthly wage.
- Compliance . There is no restriction on decreasing salaries of foreign workers, provided that the new minimum salary level continues to be met.
- Benefits and allowances . Benefits and allowances may only be included in the minimum salary calculation if they are specified in the employment contract, are guaranteed and fixed, and​ not paid in kind. Holiday allowance cannot be included in the minimum salary calculation.​
Updated November 7, 2025
EU Blue Card. Effective January 1, 2026, the minimum salary level for will be as follows:
- Non-shortage occupation: EUR 50,700, up from the current EUR 48,300.
- Shortage occupation: EUR 45,934,20, up from the current EUR 43,759.80.
- Young professionals (individuals who obtained their education qualifications in the three years prior to applying for an EU Blue Card): EUR 45,934.20 , up from the current EUR 43,759.80 .
Specialists with extensive work experience. Effective January 1, 2026, the minimum salary level will be EUR 45,630, up from the current EUR 43,470 .
Skilled Worker Permit / Local Hire Permit. Effective January 1, 2026, the minimum salary requirements for Skilled Worker Permit / Local Hire Permit applicants aged 45 and older will be EUR 55,770 per year, up from the current EUR 53,130 per year. Otherwise, applicants must evidence adequate retirement provisions.
National minimum wage. Effective January 1, 2026, the national minimum wage will increase to EUR 13.90 per hour, up from the previous EUR 12.82. It will then increase again on January 1, 2027 to 14.60. The change does not affect employment-based permit holders, who are required to receive the market salary rate for their position and qualifications.
Other relevant considerations:
- Other permit types. Under the EU Intracompany Transferee (ICT) Permit (locally called ICT Card) and Local Hire Work & Residence Permit, foreign nationals must receive a salary comparable to a local worker.
- Benefits and allowances. Contact your immigration professional for advice on benefits and allowances.
- Currency. Employers are required to guarantee the salary in EUR, since EU Blue Card applicants must be on local payroll and paid in EUR.
Updated April 1, 2026
National minimum wage. Effective April 1, 2026, the national minimum wage has increased to EUR 920 gross per month (calculated over 14 payments), up from the previous EUR 880. The increase is not expected to affect employment-based permit holders as they are primarily required to receive the market salary rate for their position.

Other salary-related considerations:
- Allowable work hours. SOC code salary thresholds for affected workers are now calculated based on a 37.5-hour work week, rather than the standard 39-hour work week.
- Thresholds for employees with fluctuating work hours. Additional guidance has been released regarding salary thresholds for workers with fluctuating work hours week-to-week. Changes include the following: work in excess of 48 hours in some weeks can be considered toward the salary threshold, provided the average over a regular cycle (which can be less than, but not more than, 17 weeks) is not more than 48 hours a week; any unpaid rest weeks will count toward the average when considering whether the salary thresholds are met; and any unpaid rest weeks will not count as absences from employment for the purpose of the rules which allow the cancellation of permission where there are absences from work for more than four weeks.

Accreditation Card: Effective August 1, 2025, the minimum salary level for the accreditation card and work permit will increase to UZS 1,271,000 per month, up from the current UZS 1,155,000.

Effective January 1, 2026, the Vietnam government increased the minimum regional wage by 7.2%. All employees who work under labor contracts under the labor code which includes businesses, organizations, cooperatives, households and individuals who hire employees will benefit from the increase.
- Regional variation . The government will increase the minimum monthly wage to the following amounts depending on the region where the employee is located: VND 5,310,000 in region 1; VND 4,730,000 in region 2; VND 4,140,000 in region 3; and VND 3,700,000 in region 4.
- Hourly pay . The government will also increase the minimum hourly wage rates to the following amounts: VND 25,500 (up from VND 23,800) per hour in region 1; VND 22,700 (up from VND 21,200) per hour in region 2; VND 20,000 (up from VND 18,600) per hour in region 3; and VND 17,800 (up from VND 16,600) per hour in region 4.
- Benefits and allowances . Benefits and allowances may not be included in the minimum salary calculation for local hires.
- Currency . Employers are required to guarantee the salary in VND regardless of payroll location and/or exchange rate fluctuations.
- Compliance . The new salary levels apply to current permit holders, pending and new applications.
- Penalties . If an employer pays less than the legal minimum wage, whether to a foreigner or a Vietnamese national, the company is subject to administrative penalties.
Monetary fines . For a company, the fine is generally double the individual amount, meaning roughly:
- VND 40–60 million VND if 1-10 employees affected;
- VND 60–100 million VND if 11-50 employees affected; and
- VND 100–150 million VND if 51+ employees affected
Required corrective actions . In addition to fines, the employer will be required to:
- Pay the difference between what was paid and the correct minimum wage
- Pay interest on the underpaid amounts (calculated at the highest non-term deposit rate announced by state banks at the time of the violation)
